message:
fix(btrfs,ondisk): use disk_num_bytes for ram_bytes in extent serialization, remove stale extent sort, and drop unused TEA hash variables

Three targeted fixes:

1. Btrfs extent data serialization (ffs-btrfs):
   Regular extent's ram_bytes field was populated from num_bytes (the
   logical/uncompressed size) instead of disk_num_bytes (the actual
   on-disk compressed size). For compressed extents, ram_bytes should
   reflect the disk footprint, not the decompressed size.

2. Remove dead btrfs extent sort (ffs-core):
   The extents.sort_by_key() call sorted extents by (generation, logical)
   before reading. This sort was incorrect for multi-extent files: extents
   should be read in logical offset order (which they already are from the
   B-tree walk), not generation order. Removing it restores correct read
   ordering for files with extents spanning multiple generations.

3. Drop unused dx_hash_tea variables (ffs-ondisk):
   The `c` and `d` variables in dx_hash_tea were initialized from seed[2]
   and seed[3] but never used in the TEA hash computation, causing dead
   code warnings.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
